step_by_step_thinking: I fully concur with agent1’s assessment of phenylbutyrate being the appropriate drug for managing ammonia levels in urea cycle disorders due to its mechanism of action which effectively bypasses the defective enzyme pathways responsible for excessive ammonia production. There isn't any evidence suggesting that the other options would have similar efficacy in this context. Therefore, my answer remains unchanged: **Option B** (phenylbutyrate). Agree.
